questions.question: Explain the difference between the 'SAMEORIGIN' and 'DENY' values for the X-Frame-Options header. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: DENY prevents the page from being framed by ANY other page, regardless of origin.
SAMEORIGIN allows the page to be framed ONLY by other pages from the exact same origin (protocol, host, port).
SAMEORIGIN is less restrictive than DENY as it permits framing within the same site.

questions.question: Describe the clickjacking attack technique and discuss two reliable server-side methods to prevent it. (8 marks)
expectedPoints: Definition: Clickjacking tricks users into interacting with a hidden UI layer (iframe) to perform unintended actions on a legitimate site.
Technique: Uses CSS (opacity:0, z-index) to overlay a transparent iframe on a decoy button.
Prevention Method 1: X-Frame-Options Header. Explain DENY/SAMEORIGIN.
Prevention Method 2: Content-Security-Policy Header with frame-ancestors directive. Explain 'none'/'self'.
Bonus: Mention that using both provides a defense-in-depth approach for browser compatibility.
